properties.remark: A WEST NORTHWEST WIND GUST OF 35 KNOTS, OR 40 MPH, WAS OBSERVED AT THE USCG STATION KEY WEST WEATHERFLOW STATION AT 127 EST.
A WEST NORTHWEST WIND GUST OF 37 KNOTS, OR 43 MPH, WAS OBSERVED BY THE KEY WEST INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT ASOS STATION AT 130 PM EST.
NAS KEY WEST - BOCA CHICA ASOS RECORDED A WEST-SOUTHWEST WIND GUST OF 48 KTS, OR 55 MPH. WINDS WERE SUSTAINED AT 31 KTS, OR 36 MPH.
CUDJOE KEY CWOP STATION, CW0925, RECORDED A WEST-SOUTHWEST WIND GUST OF 36 KTS, OR 42 MPH.
SOMBRERO KEY C-MAN STATION RECORDED A WEST-SOUTHWEST WIND GUST OF 40 KTS, OR 46 MPH.

A BRIEF, THIN WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED BY A TRAINED SPOTTER, AND OBSERVED BY THE FLORIDA KEYS NWS OFFICE, WEST-SOUTHWEST OF KEY WEST. THE WATERSPOUT WAS TILTED AT A 45-DEGREE ANGLE AND THE CONDENSATION CLOUD EXTENDED ONE-THIRD OF THE WAY DOWN FROM THE CLOUD BASE TO THE HORIZON. NO SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE AS VIEW OF THE SURFACE WAS OBSTRUCTED. MOVEMENT WAS SLOWLY TO THE EAST-SOUTHEAST AND THE DURATION WAS APPROXIMATELY 3 MINUTES.

UPDATE TO PREVIOUS WATERSPOUT REPORT FROM 4 WSW KEY WEST. A BRIEF, THIN WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED BY A TRAINED SPOTTER, AND OBSERVED BY THE FLORIDA KEYS NWS OFFICE, WEST-SOUTHWEST OF KEY WEST. THE WATERSPOUT WAS TILTED AT A 45-DEGREE ANGLE AND THE CONDENSATION CLOUD EXTENDED ONE-THIRD OF THE WAY DOWN FROM THE CLOUD BASE TO THE HORIZON. NO SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE AS VIEW OF THE SURFACE WAS OBSTRUCTED. MOVEMENT WAS SLOWLY TO THE EAST-SOUTHEAST AND THE DURATION WAS APPROXIMATELY 3 MINUTES. UPDATED INFORMATION: BETWEEN 4:51-6:25 PM THERE WAS A TOTAL OF 6 CONFIRMED WATERSPOUTS WITH 2 MORE POSSIBLE, THOUGH VISIBILITY WAS DETERIORATING DUE TO SUNSET, IN A CLOUD LINE THAT EXTENDED SE-SW OF THE EXTREME LOWER KEYS. A SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE WITH ONE OF THESE WATERSPOUTS AS IT PASSED SOUTH OF KEY WEST. MOST WERE BRIEF IN DURATION, BUT ONE SPOUT LASTED A TOTAL OF 25 MINUTES, VISIBLE ON A ROOFTOP NWS CAMERA. MOST CONDENSATION CLOUDS EXTENDED ONLY A QUARTER OF THE WAY DOWN FROM THE CLOUD BASE.
SOMBRERO KEY C-MAN STATION RECORDED A WEST WIND GUST OF 40 KTS, OR 45 MPH.

AN OFF-DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE OBSERVED 4 NEARLY-STATIONARY WATERSPOUTS SW OF CUDJOE KEY BEGINNING AROUND 9:20 AM EST. THE CONDENSATION CLOUDS WERE NARROW AND ONLY ONE EXTENDED AS FAR AS HALFWAY TO THE SURFACE. NO SPRAY RINGS WERE OBSERVED. AVERAGE DURATION WAS 8 MINUTES FOR THE FIRST THREE WATERSPOUTS, ALL DISSIPATING BY 9:36 AM, AND THE LAST ONE WAS ONGOING AT THE TIME OF THE REPORT AT 9:41 AM.

properties.city: 5 W EAST END OF LONG KE
properties.remark: A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC OBSERVED A WATERSPOUT NORTH OF DUCK KEY ALONG A CLOUD LINE. NO DETAILS WERE RELAYED IN REGARDS TO A SPRAY RING OR START/END TIME.

A FAMILY OF WATERSPOUTS WAS OBSERVED FORMING BENEATH A CLOUD LINE OVER THE GULFSIDE WATERS ADJACENT TO THE LOWER KEYS FROM THE SADDLEBUNCH KEYS TO KEY WEST BETWEEN 305 PM AND 445 PM. NUMEROUS REPORTS AND PICTURES WERE SUBMITTED BY OFF-DUTY NWS EMPLOYEES AS WELL AS THE PUBLIC EACH WATERSPOUT LASTED ABOUT 5 TO 15 MINS BEFORE DISSIPATING. NO REPORTS OF SPRAY RINGS WITH ANY OF THE WATER SPOUTS.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS WATER SPOUT REPORT FROM 4 N KEY WEST. A FAMILY OF WATERSPOUTS WAS OBSERVED FORMING BENEATH A CLOUD LINE OVER THE GULFSIDE WATERS ADJACENT TO THE LOWER KEYS FROM THE SADDLEBUNCH KEYS TO KEY WEST BETWEEN 305 PM AND 445 PM. NUMEROUS REPORTS AND PICTURES WERE SUBMITTED BY OFF-DUTY NWS EMPLOYEES AS WELL AS THE PUBLIC EACH WATERSPOUT LASTED ABOUT 5 TO 15 MINS BEFORE DISSIPATING. NO REPORTS OF SPRAY RINGS WITH ANY OF THE WATER SPOUTS. AROUND 20 WATERSPOUTS WERE OBSERVED.

A FAMILY OF WATERSPOUTS WAS OBSERVED OVER THE GULFSIDE WATERS NORTH OF STOCK ISLAND AND BOCA CHICA KEY BETWEEN 505 PM AND 534 PM. PUBLIC REPORTED THE WATERSPOUT WAS TOO FAR AWAY TO OBSERVE ANY SPRAY RINGS. LAST REPORTED WATERSPOUT OBSERVED ROPED OUT AND DISSIPATED AT 534 PM.
A WATERSPOUT WAS OBSERVED BY THE PUBLIC OVER THE CUDJOE BAY WATERS AT NEAR 801 PM. NO OBSERVED SPRAY RING. DURATION AROUND 2 MINUTES.
A WEATHERFLOW MESONET WEATHER STATION AT CAREYSFORT REEF LIGHT RECORDED A WIND GUSTS OF 35 KNOTS, OR 40 MPH, AT 457 AM EDT.
A WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS, OR 39 MPH, WAS RECORDED AT ALLIGATOR REEF LIGHT WEATHERFLOW AT 616 AM EDT.
LONG KEY C-MAN STATION RECORDED A WIND GUST OF 36 KTS, OR 41 MPH, AT 620 AM EDT.
THE WEATHERFLOW MESONET STATION AT CARYSFORT REEF LIGHT MEASURED A 41 KNOT WIND GUST DURING A RECENT SQUALL.
THE SOMBRERO KEY CMAN STATION MEASURED A 44 KNOT WIND GUST DURING A RECENT SQUALL.
THE WEATHERFLOW MESONET STATION AT ALLIGATOR REEF LIGHT MEASURED A 41 KNOT WIND GUST DURING A RECENT SQUALL.
FLORIDA KEYS NWS RSOIS RECORDED A WIND GUST OF 40 KTS, OR 46 MPH, AT 1140 PM EDT.
KEY WEST COAST GUARD WEATHERFLOW RECORDED A WIND GUST OF 40 KTS, OR 46 MPH, AT 1146 PM EDT.
KEY WEST INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT ASOS RECORDED A WIND GUST OF 40 KTS, OR 46 MPH, AT 1152 PM EDT.
NAS KEY WEST ASOS AT BOCA CHICA MEASURED A WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS, OR 39 MPH, AT 1153 PM EDT.

OBSERVATION FROM OFF-DUTY NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE PERSONNEL OBSERVED MULTIPLE WATERSPOUTS OCEANSIDE SOUTH OF ISLAMORADA. ONE OBSERVED AS THICK AND ELEPHANT-TRUNK LIKE. SEVERAL OTHER WATERSPOUTS WERE THIN.
A WATERSPOUT WAS OBSERVED BY THE PUBLIC NORTH OF MARATHON IN THE BAYSIDE WATERS AT NEAR 830 AM. NO OBSERVED SPRAY RING. DURATION AROUND 2 MINUTES.
A WATERSPOUT WAS OBSERVED BY THE PUBLIC NORTH OF MARATHON KEY IN THE BAYSIDE WATERS AT NEAR 830 AM. NO OBSERVED SPRAY RING. DURATION AROUND 2 MINUTES.
A WATERSPOUT WAS OBSERVED BY THE PUBLIC SOUTH OF CURRY HAMMOCK STATE PARK AT NEAR 945 AM. NO OBSERVED SPRAY RING. DURATION AROUND 2 MINUTES.
A WATERSPOUT WAS OBSERVED BY AN OFF-DUTY NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE METEOROLOGIST SOUTH OF KEY COLONY BEACH. NO SPRAY RING INFORMATION AVAILABLE. DURATION AROUND 6 MINUTES.
A WATERSPOUT WAS OBSERVED BY AN OFF-DUTY NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE METEOROLOGIST SOUTH OF KEY COLONY BEACH WITH A VISIBLE SPRAY RING. DURATION AROUND 6 MINUTES.
GUSTS ASSOCIATED WITH CONVECTION IN HAWK CHANNEL WERE OBSERVED BY C-MAN STATION SOUTHWEST OF MARATHON, 37 KNOTS, CONVERTED TO 43 MPH.
A SW WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS, OR 39 MPH, WAS RECORDED BY THE KEY WEST INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT ASOS AT 8:29 AM EDT.
A SW WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS, OR 39 MPH, WAS MEASURED BY THE FLORIDA KEYS NWS RSOIS AROUND 8:54 AM EDT.
A WSW WIND GUST OF 38 KNOTS, OR 44 MPH, WAS RECORDED BY THE KEY WEST INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT ASOS AT 9:56 AM EDT.
A WSW WIND GUST OF 35 KNOTS, OR 40 MPH, WAS RECORDED BY THE FLORIDA KEYS NWS RSOIS AT 958 AM EDT.
A SW WIND GUST OF 39 KNOTS, OR 45 MPH, WAS RECORDED BY THE NAS KEY WEST-BOCA CHICA ASOS AT 10:02 AM EDT.
A WEST WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS, OR 39 MPH, WAS RECORDED BY THE LONG KEY C-MAN STATION AT 11:50 AM EDT.
A WSW WIND GUST OF 37 KNOTS, OR 43 MPH, WAS RECORDED BY THE WEATHERFLOW MESONET SITE AT CARYSFORT REEF LIGHT AT 12:07 PM EDT.
A SOUTHWEST WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS, OR 39 MPH, WAS MEASURED BY THE WEATHERFLOW MESONET SITE AT ALLIGATOR REEF LIGHT AT 12:16 PM EDT.
A WEST WIND GUST OF 38 KNOTS, OR 44 MPH, WAS RECORDED BY THE WEATHERFLOW MESONET SITE AT CARYSFORT REEF LIGHT AT 12:17 PM EDT.
BRIEF WATERSPOUT WAS SPOTTED FROM THE WEBCAM LOOKING SOUTH OF WFO KEY AT 1155 AM EDT. DISTANCE WAS 1 SM SSE OF KEY WEST. WATERSPOUT DISSIPATED SHORTLY AFTER DETECTION FOLLOWED BY A BURST OF SHOWERS.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS WATER SPOUT REPORT FROM 1 SSE KEY WEST. BRIEF WATERSPOUT WAS SPOTTED FROM THE WEBCAM LOOKING SOUTH OF WFO KEY AT 1155 AM EDT. DISTANCE WAS 1 SM SSE OF KEY WEST. WATERSPOUT DISSIPATED SHORTLY AFTER DETECTION FOLLOWED BY A BURST OF SHOWERS. CORRECTION MADE TO UPDATE TIME OF OCCURRENCE.
WATERSPOUT OBSERVED VIA OFFICE WEBCAM 5 MI SW OF KEY WEST. WATERSPOUT DISSIPATED SHORTLY AFTER DETECTION.

THE OBSERVER AT NAS KEY WEST - BOCA CHICA REPORTED A WATERSPOUT NW OF KEY WEST AT 8:12 PM EDT. IT WAS ALSO OBSERVED FROM THE NWS OFFICE, WITH THE CONDENSATION CLOUD EXTENDING ABOUT ONE THIRD OF THE WAY FROM THE CLOUD BASE TO THE HORIZON. A SECOND ONE WAS OBSERVED TO THE SW OF THE ORIGINAL, WITH THE CONDENSATION CLOUD EXTENDING ABOUT ONE FIFTH OF THE WAY FROM THE CLOUD BASE TO THE HORIZON. SPRAY RINGS WERE NOT VISIBLE DUE TO AN OBSTRUCTED VIEW OF THE SURFACE. MOVEMENT WAS TO THE NE AROUND 10 KNOTS. DURATION OF THE FIRST WATERSPOUT WAS APPROXIMATELY 6 MINUTES AND ABOUT 15 MINUTES FOR THE SECOND, WITH BOTH DISSIPATING BY 8:31 PM.

A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC REPORTED A MATURE WATERSPOUT JUST EAST OF KIKIS SANDBAR ON LITTLE TORCH KEY VIA SOCIAL MEDIA. PHOTOS AND VIDEO SHOWED A NEARLY VERTICAL, NARROW CONDENSATION CLOUD EXTENDING ABOUT 80% OF THE WAY DOWN FROM THE CLOUD BASE, WITH AN APPARENT ABRUPT SHIFT NEAR THE SURFACE AS THE SPRAY RING WAS DISPLACED TO THE NORTH. MOVEMENT WAS LIKELY STATIONARY AND DURATION WAS ESTIMATED TO BE ABOUT 15 MINUTES.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS WATERSPOUT REPORT FROM 1 E LITTLE TORCH KEY TO ACCOUNT FOR A SHORTER DURATION. A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC REPORTED A MATURE WATERSPOUT JUST EAST OF KIKIS SANDBAR ON LITTLE TORCH KEY VIA SOCIAL MEDIA. PHOTOS AND VIDEO SHOWED A NEARLY VERTICAL, NARROW CONDENSATION CLOUD EXTENDING ABOUT 80% OF THE WAY DOWN FROM THE CLOUD BASE, WITH AN APPARENT ABRUPT SHIFT NEAR THE SURFACE AS THE SPRAY RING WAS DISPLACED TO THE NORTH. MOVEMENT WAS LIKELY STATIONARY AND DURATION WAS APPROXIMATELY 10 MINUTES.
A WATERSPOUT WAS OBSERVED BY AN OFF DUTY NWS METEOROLOGIST BAYSIDE OF BAHIA HONDA KEY. NO SPRAY RING OBSERVED AND DURATION UNKNOWN.
TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTS A WATERSPOUT LOCATED NW OF BOCA CHICA KEY.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS FUNNEL CLOUD REPORT FROM 2 S BIG TORCH KEY. PUBLIC REPORTS A WATERSPOUT IN BETWEEN SUMMERLAND AND CUDJOE KEY. WATERSPOUT LASTED 2-3 MINUTES AND WAS MOVING IN A WESTERLY DIRECTION. NO VISIBLE SPRAY RING AND WAS ABOUT 1/4 TO 1/3 OF THE WAY DOWN TO THE SURFACE.
PUBLIC REPORTS 3 WATERSPOUTS OCCURRING NEAR S. MARATHON ON THE WEST SIDE. SPRAY RINGS WERE VISIBLE, AS WERE TILTED CONDENSATION FUNNELS. DURATION UNKNOWN.
NWS METEOROLOGIST REPORTS A WATERSPOUT LOCATED BETWEEN STOCK ISLAND AND KEY WEST. THE SKINNY FUNNEL WAS ABOUT 1/3 OF THE WAY DOWN, NO SPRAY RING VISIBLE, AND DURATION WAS APPROXIMATELY 10 MINUTES.

SOCIAL MEDIA POST WAS SENT TO OFF-DUTY NWS METEOROLOGIST THAT IDENTIFIED TWO WATERSPOUTS JUST NORTH OF VACA KEY, NORTH OF 7 MILE GRILL, AT 925-930 AM. FORECASTERS IDENTIFIED THE CLOUDS ON VISIBLE SATELLITE IMAGERY TO CONFIRM THE TIME OF REPORT.

A MATURE WATERSPOUT SOUTH OF BOCA CHICA WAS REPORTED BY AN NWS METEOROLOGIST AT 3:19 PM EDT, AND WAS ALSO VISIBLE FROM FLORIDA KEYS NWS WEBCAMS. THE CONDENSATION CLOUD EXTENDED FROM THE CLOUD BASE TO THE SURFACE AND A SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE. MOVEMENT WAS SLOWLY TO THE SOUTHEAST AND IT DISSIPATED AROUND 3:30 PM.

NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTS A WATERSPOUT LOCATED SOUTH OF BIG PINE KEY. TRAINED SPOTTER ALSO REPORTS THE SAME AND PROVIDES MORE DETAIL: FUNNEL 1/4TH OF THE WAY DOWN, WAS NOTED TO BE SKINNY AND TILTED. DURATION OF EVENT UNKNOWN. POTENTIALLY LOCATED BETWEEN SUMMERLAND AND RAMROD KEY.

SKYWARN SPOTTER CALLED IN TO REPORT A WATERSPOUT APPROXIMATELY 5 MILES SOUTH OF LONG KEY, OBSERVED AT APPROXIMATELY 1315 PM. WATER SPOUT WAS DISSIPATED BY THE TIME THE SPOTTER GAINED ACCESS TO A PHONE.

A MATURE WATERSPOUT SOUTHWEST OF BOCA CHICA WAS REPORTED BY A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC VIA SOCIAL MEDIA. THE CONDENSATION CLOUD EXTENDED FROM THE CLOUD BASE TO THE SURFACE AND A SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE. DURATION WAS A FEW MINUTES BEFORE IT DISSIPATED.
THE LONG KEY C-MAN STATION RECORDED A WEST NORTHWEST WIND GUST OF 44 KTS, OR 51 MPH, AT 1050 PM EDT.

A thin waterspout was reported to the northwest of the Long Key Bridge at 202 PM EDT. The ongoing waterspout had an observed spray ring. The waterspout had been observed for at least five minutes at the time of the notification.

An off-duty broadcast meteorologist observed a mature waterspout north of Marathon International Airport at 210 EDT. Ongoing waterspout was well-formed, but too far away to observe a spray ring. Waterspout was observed for approximately 5 minutes at time of observation.
A marine thunderstorm wind gust of 35 knots, or 40 mph, was recorded by the Weatherflow Mesonet site at Carysfort Reef Light at 4:36 AM EDT.

A waterspout reported by the vessel Dreamcat to the south of Stock Island. This waterspout was verified via a rooftop camera at NWS Key West. The waterspout funnel was approximately ¼ of the way down, appeared to be tilted, and no spray ring was observed.
A southwest wind gust of 36 kts, or 41 mph, was recorded by the WeatherFlow mesonet at Carysfort Reef Light.
Corrects wind gust direction of previous Tstm Wnd Gst report from 8 ESE North Key Largo: A west wind gust of 36 kts, or 41 mph, was recorded by the WeatherFlow mesonet site at Carysfort Reef Light.

Report from a public observer documented a waterspout north of Seven Mile Bridge at 5 05 PM. Funnel was moderate thickness but only 1/2 of the way down with a defined spray ring. Estimated to last 5- minutes.

NWS Meteorologists observed one thick and one thin waterspout north of Marathon, FL as of 1240 PM. The thick funnel was 1/5 of the way down. The thin one was 1/2 of the way down with a developed spray ring.

Report from a public observer documented two waterspouts north of Pidgeon Key at 2 55 PM. Strongest funnel was thin and all the way down with a spray ring. Water spouts lasted 5-7 minutes, and observer lost sight at 3 PM.

Public reports 2 waterspouts located around Marathon near MM 54. One waterspout was all of the way down with a visible spray ring. The second waterspout was a skinny funnel approximately 1/8 to 1/4 of the way down with no visible spray ring. The waterspouts lasted only a few minutes.

Report from an off-duty meteorologist documented three waterspouts south of Big Pine Key at 6 PM. Funnels formed on the upshear side of developing cumulus towers, each lasting about 10 minutes. Funnels were moderately thick and about 1/3 of the way down. Spray rings were obscured from view if present.

A mature waterspout north of marathon was reported by a member of the public via social media. The condensation cloud appeared to extend from the cloud base to the surface, but a spray ring was not reported at the time.

Public reported via social media a picture of a mature waterspout north of the Seven Mile Bridge near Marathon. The waterspout was long, skinny, and a had a spray ring. The waterspout lasted approximately 30 minutes.
Two mature waterspouts were reported north of Marathon by a member of the public via social media. A spray ring was observed with one of the waterspouts. Duration was approximately 15 minutes.
Public reported via social media a timelapse of a waterspout north of Pigeon Key. The video shows a mature, skinny waterspout with a spray ring visible. Lasted approximately 10 to 15 minutes.
NWS Meteorologist reported two waterspouts in the vicinity of Little Duck Key. Both waterspouts were mature with a spray ring visible. Both were tall and skinny and lasted around 5 minutes.
The local Fire Chief reported trees and utility lines downed at around 1148 AM EDT at the oceanside of mile marker 96.3. Estimated wind gusts were up to 55 mph at that time.

A skinny, vertical waterspout was observed via NWS Key West webcams at 7:36 AM EDT, SSE of Key West. The condensation cloud extended about one quarter of the way from the cloud base to the surface and no spray ring was visible. Movement was to the NW at around 10 knots. Duration was approximately 7 minutes.

A second skinny, vertical waterspout was observed SSE of Key West via NWS Key West webcams at 7:47 AM EDT. The condensation cloud extended a fifth of the way from the cloud base to the surface and no spray ring was visible. Movement was to the NW at about 10 knots and duration was a brief 2 minutes.

NWS Employee and Public report a waterspout in proximity Cudjoe and Sugarloaf Key, near MM 20. Funnel was skinny, tilted, appeared to be all the way down, but a spray ring was not visible. Duration of event unknown.

A large waterspout was observed over Cudjoe Bay at 7:22 PM EDT, with the condensation cloud extending from the cloud base to the surface. A spray ring was not visible. Two additional waterspouts were also observed in this area but did not extend far from the cloud base. Movement was to the SW around 5 knots. Duration was approximately 5 minutes.

A skinny, vertical waterspout was observed via NWS Key West webcams at 7:38 PM EDT. View of the surface was obscured but the condensation cloud extended from the cloud base to the horizon. A spray ring was not visible. Movement was to the SW around 5 knots and the duration was approximately 6 minutes.

A skinny, slightly tilted waterspout was observed by the NAS Key West - Boca Chica observer at 7:42 PM EDT. The condensation cloud extended from the cloud base to the surface and a spray ring was confirmed via social media photos. Movement was to the SW around 5 knots and the duration was approximately 3 minutes.

A skinny waterspout, tilted at about a 45-degree angle, was observed by the NAS Key West - Boca Chica observer and NWS Key West meteorologists at 8:16 PM EDT. The condensation cloud extended from the cloud base to the horizon and no spray ring was visible. Movement was to the SW around 5 knots. A second waterspout formed nearby but only extended about a fifth of the way from the cloud base to the horizon. Duration between the two waterspouts was approximately 10 minutes, with the second one dissipating at 8:26 PM.
A southeast wind gust of 35 knots or 40 mph, was recorded by the WeatherFlow mesonet at Alligator Reef Light.

A skinny, rope-like waterpsout was detected on camera by NWS meteorologist west of Key West at 4:45 PM EDT. Condensation cloud extended from the cloud base to halfway down. No spray ring was visible. Duration was approximately 10 minutes.
Key West International Airport ASOS measured a wind gust of 52 mph at 744 PM EDT associated with a severe thunderstorm.
Sand Key C-MAN station measured a wind gust of 44 kts or 51 mph at 810 PM associated with a severe thunderstorm.
A north-northwest wind gust of 44 knots, or 51 mph, was recorded by the WeathFlow Mesonet Station at Carysfort Reef Light.
A north-northeast wind gust of 36 knots, or 41 mph, was measured by the WeatherFlow Mesonet station at Alligator Reef Light.
Corrects previous tstm wnd gst report from 9 SW Key West. The Sand Key C-MAN station measured a wind gust of 36 kts, or 41 mph, associated with a thunderstorm.
The Sand Key C-MAN station measured a wind gust of 36 kts, or 41 mph, associated with a severe thunderstorm.
The Sombrero Key C-MAN station measured a wind gust of 37 kts, or 43 mph, associated with a nearby thunderstorm.
A southeasterly wind gust of 34 kts, or 39 mph, was recorded by the WeatherFlow Mesonet Station at Carysfort Reef Light at 456 PM EDT.
Tiny cone shaped funnel cloud, no spray ring visible, duration unknown.

Local resident observed a waterspout via social media located southeast of Naval Air Station Key West at 1045 AM. The waterspout was 1/4 of the way down. No spray ring was observed. Duration was unknown.
Local resident filmed a waterspout southeast of Islamorada at 645 EDT. Waterspout length to ocean surface was unknown. A spray ring was observed. Duration was unknown.

A skinny waterspout was observed by a member of the public in the Gulf side waters off of mile marker 39 on US 1. The waterspout was very short lived estimated to have lasted only a few minutes with no spray ring observed at the surface.

An off-duty NWS employee observed a dust devil in the median of US1 on Boca Chica Key around 2:05 PM EDT. It exceeded 50 feet in height and appeared to cross US1 to the WNW towards the NAS Key West - Boca Chica wastewater treatment facility. Duration is unknown and no damage was reported.
A SSE wind gust of 36 knots, or 41 mph, was recorded by the WeatherFlow mesonet site at Alligator Reef Light at 9:11 PM EDT.
A NE wind gust of 38 knots, or 44 mph, was recorded by the Sand Key C-MAN station at 10:00 PM EDT.
A south wind gust of 34 knots, or 39 mph, was recorded by the WeatherFlow mesonet site at Carysfort Reef Light at 2:16 PM EDT.
A SE wind gust of 39 knots, or 44 mph, was recorded by the WeatherFlow mesonet site at Alligator Reef Light at 2:26 PM EDT.

A skinny, nearly vertical waterspout was observed via Florida Keys NWS webcams SSE of Key West. The condensation cloud extended from the cloud base to the surface and a spray ring was visible for several minutes. Movement was slowly to the west and duration was estimated to be about 10 minutes, dissipating at 3:08 PM EDT.
A SE wind gust of 37 knots, or 43 mph, was recorded by the Long Key C-MAN station at 3:00 PM EDT.

A thin waterspout observed directly south of Key West in Hawk Channel by off-duty NWS employee. Waterspout was confirmed on rooftop office webcam. Waterspout extended all the way down to the surface and a spray ring extended approximately 1/4 the way up the condensation funnel. Waterspout was observed moving on shore near Higgs beach and quickly dissipated thereafter. Waterspout lasted approximately 10 minutes.

A thin waterspout observed southeast of the Dry Tortugas National Park by an employee of park. Water. The waterspout was very short lived estimated to have lasted only a few minutes no spray ring observed at the surface.

Local resident photographed a waterspout southeast of Key Largo at approximately 530 PM. The condensation funnel was 1/4 of the way down. The waterspout was too far away to determine the presence of a spray ring. Duration was unknown.
A ESE wind gust of 34 kts, or 39 mph, was recorded by the Long Key C-MAN station at 8:45 PM EDT.
Corrects previous tstm wnd gst report from 9 SE Ocean Reef. A SSE wind gust of 39 knots, or 45 mph, was recorded by the Carysfort Reef WeatherFlow station at 10:21 PM EDT.

Corrects previous tstm wnd gst report from 6 SSW Marathon. A SE wind gust of 37 knots, or 43 mph, was measured by the Sombrero Key CMAN Station at 11:50 AM EDT.

A report at 1231 to the 911 call center at Key West reported a tree blown over at Petronia and Simonton Street. The tree blew over onto a car. No injuries were observed. Tree was blown over after passage of a severe convective rainband.

A S wind gust of 40 knots, or 46 mph, was measured by the CMAN station on Carysfort Reef Light at 5:01 PM EDT.
A SW wind gust of 48 knots, or 55 mph, was measured by the CMAN station on Carysfort Reef Light at 5:51 PM EDT.
Waterspout caught on NWS office webcam and reported by the public. Funnel was in the decay stage, appeared tilted, sheared, and approximately 1/3 of the way down.
An ENE wind gust of 35 knots, or 40 mph, was measured by the Long Key C-MAN station at 210 pm EDT.
An E wind gust of 34 knots, or 39 mph, was measured by the Sombrero Key Light C-MAN station at 11:30 pm EDT.

Two waterspouts were reported between Wisteria Island and Fleming Key at 4:57 PM EDT. The condensation clouds did not extend far from the cloud base and no spray rings were visible. Movement was slowly to the west and duration was estimated to be around 5 minutes.

A family of 4 waterspouts were observed north of Key West by NWS employees and the Weather Observer at NAS Key West - Boca Chica, beginning around 7:25 PM EDT. Two spray rings were observed via NWS Key West webcams. One was with a very skinny, nearly vertical condensation cloud that extended almost completely from the cloud base to the surface. The second with a very wide elephant trunk-like waterspout whose condensation cloud extended about one quarter of the way from the cloud base to the surface. The remaining two waterspouts were nearly vertical, very skinny, and the condensation clouds did not extend far from the cloud base. Movement of these waterspouts was to the west around 5 knots. Duration of the 4 waterspouts was approximately 21 minutes.

A waterspout was observed south of the Saddlebunch Keys by the Weather Observer at NAS Key West - Boca Chica around 530 pm EDT. The waterspout was in the dissipation stage during the time it was observed and only lasted for about 1 minute.

An off-duty NWS employee observed a waterspout looking east-southeast south of Stock Island and Boca Chica at 1005 AM EDT. The waterspout was in its dissipation stage at time of observation and webcam footage from WFO KEY observed dissipation after a few minutes. Condensation funnel was thin near the cloud, no spray ring was observed.

One waterspout was observed 0.5 miles west-southwest of Sunset Key at 12:07 PM EDT. The condensation funnel did not extend far from the cloud base and no spray rings were visible. Waterspout did not last long and was gone just after NWS employees were able to confirm the waterspout on office webcam.
Mesonet station XALG at Alligator Reef Light reported a 35 mph/31 kt wind gust at 426 PM EDT.

An off-duty National Weather Service meteorologist observed a waterspout south of Big Pine Key on the upshear side of a developing thunderstorm. The initial funnel was short and straight, followed by an extended decay stage with a fully visible, yet skinny funnel. Confirmation of spray ring was obscured by the horizon. Location determined via radar imagery.
A NW wind gust of 36 kts, or 41 mph, was recorded by the WeatherFlow mesonet site at Carysfort Reef Light at 8:51 PM EDT.

NWS Employee reported a waterspout near Spanish Harbor Key and Big Pine Key. Funnel was all the way down and had a visible spray ring. Approximate duration of event was somewhere between 10-15 minutes.
A N wind gust of 46 kts, or 53 mph, was recorded by the WeatherFlow mesonet site at Carysfort Reef Light at 9:01 PM EDT.
A NE wind gust of 34 kts, or 39 mph, was measured by the Sand Key Light C-MAN station at 11:20 PM EDT.

An official observer at Boca Chica NAS reported a waterspout on the east side of a developing shower. Initial funnel was thin and straight, only visible near the cloud base. It lasted only a few minutes with no spray ring observed. Location estimated from position spotted from NWS Key West roof webcam and radar imagery.

A NWS Employee spotted a waterspout on the west side of an ongoing shower. The short lived waterspout was initially thin and straight before gradually tilting and dissipating lasting around 5 minutes. Funnel was only visible near the cloud base with no spray ring observed. Location estimated from NWS Key West roof camera and radar imagery.
Sand Key had a gust to 39 mph or 34kts around 310 am early this morning.

A waterspout was observed by an off-duty Florida Keys NWS employee over the Atlantic waters off MM107 in Key Largo. The condensation cloud extended about a third of the way from the cloud base to the horizon. View of the surface was obstructed so no spray ring was visible. Movement was slowly to the west. Duration unknown, as it moved out of view.

A waterspout was observed via Florida Keys NWS webcams to the SW of Key West. The condensation cloud extended about one third of the way from the cloud base to the surface and no spray ring was visible. Just prior to dissipating it was tilted more than 45 degrees as a shower approached from the east. Movement was to the west around 5 knots. Duration was approximately 5 minutes.
A 1-minute wind speed of 41 kts, or 47 mph, was recorded by the Sand Key C-MAN station at 7:52 PM EDT.
A southwesterly wind gust of 51 kts, or 59 mph, was recorded by the Key West International Airport ASOS at 8:17 PM EDT.
A southwesterly wind gust of 39 kts, or 45 mph, was recorded by the WeatherFlow mesonet site at Alligator Reef Light at 10:36 PM EDT.
An easterly wind gust of 54 kts, or 62 mph, was recorded by the WeatherFlow mesonet site at Alligator Reef Light at 10:46 PM EDT.
A south-southeasterly wind gust of 43 kts, or 49 mph, was recorded by the Long Key C-MAN station at 10:50 PM EDT. A 1-minute wind speed of 37 kts, or 43 mph, was recorded at 10:41 PM.

Two waterspouts were observed by an off-duty NAS Key West - Boca Chica weather observer south of Lower Matecumbe Key this afternoon, with at least one spray ring visible. Movement was to the NW around 10 knots and the duration was at least 12 minutes, prior to moving out of sight.
A southeasterly wind gust of 34 kts, or 39 mph, was recorded by the WeatherFlow mesonet site at the U.S. Coast Guard Sector Key West at 6:00 AM EDT.
A southerly wind gust of 37 kts, or 43 mph, was recorded by the Sand Key C-MAN station at 6:00 AM EDT. The wind gust rose from 34 kts at 5:55 AM.

A vertical, mature, and elephant trunk-like waterspout was observed south of Big Pine Key around 4:54 PM EDT. The condensation cloud extended from the cloud base to the horizon over a nearby roof. View of the surface was obstructed. Observer estimated it was south of Coupon Bight; location estimated via radar. Movement was to the NE around 15 knots. Duration was at least 10-15 minutes prior to moving out of sight.
A southerly wind gust of 34 kts, or 39 mph, was recorded by the WeatherFlow mesonet site at Alligator Reef Light at 06:11 AM EDT.

Corrects previous waterspout report from 2 S Duck Key. An off-duty meteorologist observed a skinny waterspout southeast of Curry Hammock State Park at 11:56 AM EDT. Funnel was about a quarter of the way down and no spray ring was observed, duration was around 10 minutes.

Corrects previous waterspout report from 2 S Duck Key to add movement and update duration. An NWS meteorologist observed a skinny waterspout southeast of Curry Hammock State Park at 11:56 AM EDT. Funnel was about a quarter of the way down from the cloud base to the surface and no spray ring was observed. Movement was to the west around 10 knots and duration was approximately 19 minutes.
An off-duty meteorologist observed a skinny waterspout southeast of Curry Hammock State Park at 12:00 PM EDT. No spray ring was observed and duration was a few minutes.
NWS Employee reports a waterspout just north of Key West, which was also observed from our the office rooftop camera. Funnel was about halfway down and duration was unknown.

Public reports a waterspout located in the vicinity of Sugarloaf Key, beyond Lois Key. Photos depict a slightly tilted funnel that descended one-third of the way to the surface, no spray ring was visible, and duration was unknown.

Corrects previous waterspout report from 3 ESE Big Coppitt Key. Off-duty NWS employee reports a waterspout SE of Cudjoe Key. Funnel stayed vertical and never got beyond one fifth of the way to the surface. Event lasted for approximately 14 minutes, between 545-559 pm EDT.
Off-duty NWS employee reports a waterspout ESE of Big Coppitt Key. Funnel was about two-thirds of the way down. No spray ring observed. Duration unknown.

A thin, short-lived waterspout was reported by a trained spotter about 5 miles east of Boca Chica Key. The spout condensate reached around 1/4 of the way towards the surface, with no observed spray ring. The spout lasted for around 3 minutes.

Area webcams captured a skinny waterspout east of Duck Key. The condensation funnel reached the surface and a spray ring was visible. This waterspout formed upshear from and not connected to a long track, yet weak wall cloud.

Corrects previous waterspout report from 2 ESE Duck Key. Area webcams captured a skinny waterspout east of Duck Key. The condensation funnel reached the surface and a spray ring was visible. This waterspout formed upshear from and not connected to a long track, yet weak wall cloud. Correction: Local resident notified WFO KEY that the waterspout dissipated at 2:55 PM.
A social media post captured the spray ring associated with a waterspout near a local business on Little Torch Key. Duration unknown.
The Weatherflow mesonet at Alligator Reef recently measured a pair of 35 knot wind gusts during passing squalls. The earlier squall was at 416 AM.
Corrects previous tstm wnd gst report from 7 SE Ocean Reef. The Weatherflow mesonet at Carysfort Reef recently measured a 37 knot wind gust during a passing squall.
The Weatherflow mesonet at Carysfort Reef recently measured a 37 knot wind gust during a passing squall.
The Weatherflow Mesonet at Carysfort Reef Light measured a wind gust of 36 knots, or 41 mph, at 10:46 PM EST.
The Weatherflow Mesonet at Carysfort Reef Light measured a east southeast wind gust of 34 knots, or 39 mph, at 2:56 AM with a passing squall.
The WeatherFlow Mesonet station at Alligator Reef Light measured an east wind gust of 36 knots, or 41 mph, at 5:21 am.
An east southeast wind gust of 38 knots, or 44 mph, was measured by the C-MAN station located at Sombrero Key Light at 9:30 AM EST.
The WeatherFlow Mesonet station at Alligator Reef Light measured an east wind gust of 37 knots, or 43 mph, at 9:41 am.
The WeatherFlow Mesonet station at Carysfort Reef Light measured an East wind gust of 37 knots, or 43 mph, at 10:01 am EST.
The Weatherflow Mesonet station at Alligator Reef Light measured a 40 knot, or 46 mph, wind gust at 231 PM.
The Weatherflow Mesonet station at Carysfort Reef Light measured a 41 knot, or 47 mph, wind gust at 456 PM.
The Weatherflow Mesonet station at Carysfort Reef Light measured a 51 knot, or 59 mph, wind gust at 606 PM.
The Weatherflow Mesonet station at Carysfort Reef Light measured a 53 knot, or 61 mph, wind gust at 626 PM.
The Weatherflow Mesonet station at Carysfort Reef Light measured a 56 knot, or 64 mph, wind gust at 735 PM.
The C-Man station at Sombrero Key Light measured a 40 knot, or 46 mph, wind gust out of the NNE at 820 PM.
The Weatherflow Mesonet station at Carysfort Reef Light measured a 53 knots, or 61 mph, wind gust out of the E at 901 PM.
The C-Man station at Long Key measured a 40 knot, or 46 mph, wind gust out of the N at 1050 PM.
The C-Man station at Long Key measured a 49 knot, or 57 mph, wind gust out of the N at 1210 AM.
The Weatherflow Mesonet station at Alligator Reef Light measured a 47 knots, or 54 mph, wind gust out of the NNE at 1251 AM.
The Weatherflow Mesonet station at Alligator Reef Light measured a 42 knots, or 48 mph, wind gust out of the NNE at 201 AM.
The Weatherflow Mesonet station at Carysfort Reef Light measured a 75 knots, or 86 mph, wind gust out of the NE at 331 AM. Sustained winds at the time were measured at 67 knots, or 77 mph.

A funnel cloud was reported by a trained spotter about 5 miles south of Boca Chica Key. The spout did not contain a spray ring. A call at 303 PM reported a second waterspout formed east of the original one which lasted approximately 5 minutes.
An off-duty NWS employee observed a waterspout approximately 5 miles south of Key West. A spray ring was not reported or observed at this time.

The Monroe County Fire Department reports a waterspout located about 3 miles offshore on the Atlantic side near MM 32. Condensation funnel extended about 1/4 of the way down from the cloud base at the time of report.
NWS Employee observed a waterspout from the office rooftop camera. Condensation funnel extended about 1/3 of the way down and no spray ring was observed. Event lasted approximately 2 minutes.
A wind gust of 38 knots, or 44 mph, was measured by the Sand Key Light C-MAN station at 7:20 am.
A wind gust of 38 knots, or 44 mph, was measured by the Sombrero Key Light C-MAN station at 830 am EST.
